title: 比...更
prompt_en: Practice comparatives with -er and more + than.
prompt_zh: 练习比较级：-er + than / more + than。
description: 掌握比较级结构和常见不规则变化。
knowledge_points: 比较级 er than more than better
teaching_markdown:
## 比较级构成
- 短词 + er：big → bigger, fast → faster
- 以 y 结尾 → ier：happy → happier, easy → easier
- 长词 + more：more interesting, more beautiful
- 不规则：good→better, bad→worse, far→farther

## 比较句型
- A is bigger than B.（A比B大）
- A is more expensive than B.

## 常见错误
❌ He is more taller. → 不重复比较级
✅ He is taller. / He is much taller.

title: 最...的
prompt_en: Practice superlatives with the -est and the most.
prompt_zh: 练习最高级：the -est / the most。
description: 掌握最高级结构和用法。
knowledge_points: 最高级 the est the most best
teaching_markdown:
## 最高级构成
- the + 短词 + est：the biggest, the fastest
- the most + 长词：the most beautiful
- 不规则：good→best, bad→worst

## 最高级句型
- This is the best restaurant in town.
- She is the tallest in our class.

## 常见错误
❌ She is best student. → ✅ the best student
❌ the most happiest → ✅ the happiest

title: 不规则比较
prompt_en: Practice irregular comparatives like better worse more less.
prompt_zh: 练习不规则比较级：better/worse/more/less/farther。
description: 掌握不规则比较级和最高级变化。
knowledge_points: 不规则比较 better worse more less farther
teaching_markdown:
## 不规则比较级
- good → better → best
- bad → worse → worst
- many/much → more → most
- little → less → least
- far → farther/further → farthest/furthest

## 不规则比较句型
- This restaurant is better than that one.
- He is the best player on the team.
- I have more books than you.

title: 越...越...
prompt_en: Practice the + comparative the + comparative structure.
prompt_zh: 练习the...the...比较结构（越…越…）。
description: 掌握the more the more句型。
knowledge_points: 越越比较 the more the better
teaching_markdown:
## the...the... 比较结构
- The faster, the better.（越快越好）
- The more, the merrier.（越多越开心）

## 完整句型
- The more you practice, the better you get.
- The sooner, the better.
- The cheaper, the better.
- The earlier, the easier.

title: 多音节比较
prompt_en: Practice multi-syllable adjectives with more and most.
prompt_zh: 练习多音节形容词的比较级和最高级（more/most）。
description: 掌握多音节形容词比较规则。
knowledge_points: 多音节 more most 形容词比较
teaching_markdown:
## 多音节形容词比较
- 多音节（≥2音节）用 more/most
- beautiful → more beautiful → the most beautiful
- expensive → more expensive → the most expensive

## 常见多音节形容词
- important / difficult / interesting
- popular / delicious / comfortable
- dangerous / exciting / convenient

## 例句
- This phone is more expensive than that one.
- Chinese is more difficult than English.

title: 有...在...
prompt_en: Practice there is/are to describe existence.
prompt_zh: 练习there is/are存在句表达。
description: 掌握there is/are肯定否定疑问形式。
knowledge_points: there is there are 存在 位置
teaching_markdown:
## There is / There are
- There is + 单数/不可数：There is a book on the desk.
- There are + 复数：There are three people in the room.

## 否定与疑问
- 否定：There isn't / There aren't
- 疑问：Is there...? / Are there...?

## 过去存在
- There was a big storm last night.

title: 在...里/上/旁边
prompt_en: Practice prepositions of place in on at under next to.
prompt_zh: 练习位置介词：in/on/under/next to/between等。
description: 掌握常用位置介词的用法。
knowledge_points: 介词 位置 in on under next to between
teaching_markdown:
## 常用位置介词
- in（在…里面）：in the box
- on（在…上面）：on the table
- under（在…下面）：under the chair
- next to（在…旁边）：next to the bank
- between（在…之间）：between A and B
- across from（在…对面）：across from the station

## 常见错误
❌ The cat is in the table. → ✅ on the table

title: 方向与指路
prompt_en: Practice giving directions go straight turn left right.
prompt_zh: 练习指路表达：直走/左转/右转/过马路。
description: 掌握基本方向指路用语。
knowledge_points: 指路 方向 go straight turn left right
teaching_markdown:
## 方向指路
- Go straight and turn left at the corner.
- Turn right at the traffic light.
- It's on your left/right.
- Go past the bank.

## 常用表达
- Go straight ahead.
- Take the first right.
- Cross the street.
- It's at the end of the street.

title: 空间描述
prompt_en: Practice describing spatial relationships between objects.
prompt_zh: 练习描述物体之间的空间关系。
description: 掌握空间描述常用句型。
knowledge_points: 空间 位置 介词 描述
teaching_markdown:
## 空间描述
- The restaurant is between the hotel and the cinema.
- The hospital is across from the station.
- The bank is next to the post office.
- The keys are in my bag.

## 楼层表达
- It's on the second floor.
- It's on the first floor.
- It's on the top floor.

title: 存在句疑问否定
prompt_en: Practice there is/are questions and negative forms.
prompt_zh: 练习there is/are的疑问句和否定句。
description: 掌握存在句的疑问和否定用法。
knowledge_points: 疑问 否定 Is there there isn''t any
teaching_markdown:
## 存在句疑问
- Is there a bank near here? — Yes, there is.
- Are there any questions? — No, there aren't.

## 否定存在
- There isn't any milk left.
- There aren't any seats left.

## some vs any
- 肯定：There is some water.
- 否定/疑问：There isn't any water.

title: 和...一样
prompt_en: Practice as...as comparatives for equal comparison.
prompt_zh: 练习as...as同级比较表达相同程度。
knowledge_points: 同级比较 as as 一样
teaching_markdown:
## as...as 同级比较
- as + 形容词 + as：和…一样
- She is as tall as her sister.
- This car is as fast as that one.

## as many/much as
- I have as many books as you.
- He has as much money as me.

## 例句
- He is as smart as you.
- This book is as good as that one.

title: 不如...
prompt_en: Practice not as...as for negative comparison.
prompt_zh: 练习not as...as表达"不如…"的比较。
knowledge_points: 不如 not as as 否定比较
teaching_markdown:
## not as...as 否定比较
- not as + 形容词 + as：不如…
- This car is not as expensive as that one.
- He is not as tall as his brother.

## 例句
- Today is not as cold as yesterday.
- This phone is not as good as that one.
- She doesn't run as fast as I do.

title: 太...不能
prompt_en: Practice too + adjective to express excess.
prompt_zh: 练习too+形容词表达"太…而不能…"。
knowledge_points: too 太 不能 程度
teaching_markdown:
## too + 形容词
- too 表示太…"，超过可接受的程度

scene_title: - This bag is too heavy. I can't carry it.
- This coffee is too hot to drink.
- too big / too small / too expensive
- too hot / too cold / too noisy
- too difficult / too easy / too far
- This shirt is too small for me.

title: 足够...
prompt_en: Practice enough adjective to express sufficiency.
prompt_zh: 练习形容词+enough表达"足够…"。
knowledge_points: enough 足够 程度 位置
teaching_markdown:
## 形容词 + enough
- enough 放在形容词后
- The room is big enough for ten people.
- I'm not tall enough to reach the top shelf.

## 名词 + enough
- enough 放在名词前
- I have enough money.
- There isn't enough time.

## 例句
- This car is fast enough for me.
- She is old enough to drive.
- The water is warm enough to swim.

title: 方式副词
prompt_en: Practice adverbs of manner quickly carefully well hard.
prompt_zh: 练习方式副词：quickly/carefully/well/hard/slowly。
description: 掌握方式副词的构成和位置。
knowledge_points: 副词 方式 quickly well carefully
teaching_markdown:
## 方式副词
- 形容词 + ly：quick → quickly, careful → carefully
- 特殊：good → well, fast → fast, hard → hard
- y → ily：happy → happily, easy → easily

## 副词位置
- 动词后：She speaks English well.
- 句尾：He drives carefully.

## 常见副词
- slowly / quickly / carefully / quietly
- well / hard / fast / clearly
- politely / happily / easily

title: 偏好选择
prompt_en: Practice expressing preferences which do you like more I prefer.
prompt_zh: 练习偏好表达：Which do you like more / I prefer / I like better。
knowledge_points: 偏好 prefer like better which more
teaching_markdown:
## 偏好选择
- Which do you like more, A or B?
- I like A better.
- I prefer A (to B).

## 常见表达
- I prefer this restaurant. It's cheaper.
- Which do you like more, tea or coffee?
- Between these two, which is faster?

## 例句
- I like both, but I like this one more.
- This one is more comfortable. I'll take this.

title: 推荐表达
prompt_en: Practice making recommendations I recommend this is the best.
prompt_zh: 练习推荐表达：I recommend / This is the best / You should try。
knowledge_points: 推荐 recommend the best you should
teaching_markdown:
## 推荐表达
- I recommend this one.
- This is the best restaurant in this area.
- You should try it.

## 理由
- It's the most popular dish here.
- It's not the cheapest, but it's the most reliable.
- If you want something cheap, this is your best choice.

## 例句
- I recommend this book. It's very interesting.
- This is the best café in the neighborhood.

title: 对比评价
prompt_en: Practice comparison evaluation compared to the same but.
prompt_zh: 练习对比评价：Compared to / It's the same but / I've tried both。
knowledge_points: 对比 compared to same price tried both
teaching_markdown:
## 对比评价
- Compared to the other one, this is much better.
- It's the same price, but this one is more useful.
- I've tried both, and this one is definitely better.

## 例句
- Compared to my old phone, this one is much faster.
- It's the same size, but this one is lighter.
- I've tried both restaurants, and this one is better.

title: 最高级推荐
prompt_en: Practice superlative recommendations the best in...
prompt_zh: 练习最高级推荐：the best in / the most popular。
knowledge_points: 最高级推荐 the best the most recommend
teaching_markdown:
## 最高级推荐
- This is the best restaurant in this area.
- It's the most popular dish here.
- It's not the cheapest, but it's the most reliable.

## 例句
- This is the best park in the city.
- She is the most talented singer in the group.
- This is the most comfortable hotel I've stayed at.

title: 二选一比较
prompt_en: Practice comparing two options between A and B.
prompt_zh: 练习二选一比较：Between A and B
knowledge_points: 掌握二选一比较句型。
teaching_markdown: 二选一 between which more better
ink_script_key:
## 二选一比较
- Between A and B, which is faster?
- Which do you like more, A or B?
- I like this one a little more because it's brighter.

## 例句
- Between the bus and the subway, which is cheaper?
- Between these two, which is better for beginners?
- I like both, but I prefer this one because it's more practical.

title: 多维度比较
prompt_en: Practice multi-dimensional comparisons with and but.
prompt_zh: 练习多维度比较：更…但…（比较多个维度）。
knowledge_points: 多维度比较 lighter but better camera
teaching_markdown:
## 多维度比较
- This phone is lighter and faster, but that one has a better camera.
- This restaurant is more expensive, but the food is much better.

## 常用结构
- A is ___er and ___, but B has ___.
- A is ___, but B is ___.

## 例句
- This laptop is cheaper and lighter, but that one has more storage.
- Hotel A is more expensive, but it's closer to the beach.

title: 变化描述
prompt_en: Practice describing changes with getting more and more.
prompt_zh: 练习变化描述：越来越…、变得更好等。
knowledge_points: 变化 getting warmer more and more becoming
teaching_markdown:
## 变化描述
- It's getting hotter and hotter every day.
- He is more and more confident.
- The city is becoming more beautiful than before.

## 常用结构
- getting + 比较级 + and + 比较级
- more and more + 形容词
- becoming + 比较级 + than before

## 例句
- The prices are getting higher and higher.
- She is becoming more and more popular.

title: 性价比评价
prompt_en: Practice value for money expressions worth the price.
prompt_zh: 练习性价比评价：便宜但质量、值得、一分钱一分货。
knowledge_points: 性价比 cheaper quality worth get what you pay
teaching_markdown:
## 性价比评价
- This one is cheaper, but the quality is not as good.
- You get what you pay for.
- The more you pay, the better quality you get.

## 例句
- This is good value for money.
- It's not the cheapest, but it's the most durable.
- Don't buy the cheapest one if you want something good.

title: 综合推荐
prompt_en: Practice comprehensive recommendations all things considered.
prompt_zh: 练习综合推荐：综合考虑/整体来看/强烈推荐。
knowledge_points: 综合推荐 all things considered I highly recommend
teaching_markdown:
## 综合推荐
- All things considered, this is the best choice for beginners.
- I highly recommend this product.
- It's not the cheapest, but it's the most durable.

## 例句
- All things considered, this is the best option.
- I highly recommend this hotel. It's comfortable and affordable.
- Overall, this is the best value for money.

title: 观点对比
prompt_en: Practice comparing opinions in my opinion however.
prompt_zh: 练习观点对比：依我看…但有些人认为…。
knowledge_points: 观点对比 in my opinion however prefer
teaching_markdown:
## 观点对比
- In my opinion, this one is better.
- However, some people prefer that one because it's cheaper.

## 常用结构
- In my opinion / From my perspective / Personally
- However / On the other hand
- Some people prefer...because...

## 例句
- In my opinion, this is the best choice. However, some people might disagree.
- Some people prefer city life because it's convenient. However, I prefer the suburbs.
